content.json:
# mdk - eval and drift workbook

**Persona:** eval engineer / model owner ("is the model behaving today?").

## Honest disclaimer (read first)

> **TODO - eval/drift telemetry is NOT in Application Insights today.**
>
> Per ADR 031 D1 and `docs/observability.md`, `mdk eval` emits **Langfuse scores**, not OTel metrics. There are no `mdk.eval.*` or `drift.*` instruments in `METRIC_NAMES` (`src/movate/tracing/metrics.py`), so the workspace has nothing to query.
>
> This workbook is **scaffolded** for when ADR 016 (model promotion / canary) instruments and ADR 031 follow-ups land: each query below is the **shape** the panel will use, filtered against `AppMetrics`/`AppTraces` with the expected `Name` patterns. Until then, treat this workbook as documentation of intent + a forward-compatible template.
>
> **Where to actually look today:**
> - Eval pass-rate / regression: Langfuse UI, scores keyed by `run_id` (ADR 015).
> - Drift signal: Langfuse + the operator workbook's latency/error trends as a proxy.
> - Canary status: Container Apps revision health (see the platform workbook's revisions section).

Scope to the Log Analytics workspace once the instruments exist; the workbook structure stays the same.

## 1. Eval pass-rate over time (scaffolded)

**What this WILL measure (when instrumented).** A counter or score for `mdk.eval.pass` / `mdk.eval.fail` partitioned by `agent` / `eval_set`. Plotted as pass-fraction over time.

**Normal (target).** > 95% pass-rate per agent + eval-set; trend flat.

**If red, do (target playbook).**
- pass-rate stepped down -> bisect on prompt / model / KB chunks since the last green run.
- drift on a specific eval-set only -> KB regression for the topic that set covers.

_The query below is the shape: once the eval-runner emits an instrument named `mdk.eval.<x>`, drop the empty-result banner and the chart populates._

content.title: Eval pass-rate (%) by agent / eval_set [empty until ADR 031 follow-up lands]

content.json:
## 2. Drift signal (scaffolded)

**What this WILL measure.** Drift instruments under `drift.*` (input distribution, output distribution, embedding distance vs baseline). Today these are not emitted - the closest proxy is the operator workbook's latency / error rate trend.

**Normal (target).** All drift metrics within 1 standard deviation of the rolling baseline.

**If red, do (target playbook).**
- input-distribution drift -> upstream input change (real-world shift, or upstream system change).
- embedding-distance drift on a specific KB index -> KB content rotation; verify chunk pipeline.
- output drift only -> prompt / model swap; verify with `mdk eval`.

_Same shape: empty until instrumented._

content.query:
// PLACEHOLDER - drift instruments not in METRIC_NAMES today.
// When instrumented: AppMetrics where Name startswith "drift.".
AppMetrics
| where Name startswith "drift."
| extend signal = tostring(split(Name, ".")[1])
| summarize Value = avg(Sum) by signal, bin(TimeGenerated, 15m)
| order by TimeGenerated asc
content.title: Drift signals over time [empty until ADR 016 D3 / ADR 031 follow-up lands]

content.json:
## 3. Canary status (ADR 016 D3)

**What this WILL show.** Active canary deployments per agent (`agent_version_canary` vs `agent_version_stable`) and the win/loss verdict from the canary comparison job. Today the canary lifecycle is in design (ADR 016 D3); the runtime emits the `agent_version` attribute on `agent.execute` already, so once the comparison job lands, this panel populates from existing telemetry.

**Normal (target).** No active canary, OR canary on a single agent with green verdict.

**If red, do (target playbook).**
- canary loss -> auto-revert (per ADR 016 D3); confirm the revert happened in the platform workbook's revisions section.
- canary stuck without verdict -> the comparison job didn't run; check eval pipeline.

content.query:
// Today: just lists distinct agent_version values seen on agent.execute.
// Useful as a smoke check that the version attribute is being stamped at the runtime edge.
AppDependencies
| where Name == "agent.execute" and AppRoleName == "movate-runtime"
| extend agent = tostring(Properties["agent"]), agent_version = tostring(Properties["agent_version"])
| summarize Runs = count(), P95 = percentile(DurationMs, 95), ErrorPct = round(100.0 * countif(Success == false) / count(), 2) by agent, agent_version
| order by Runs desc
| take 50
content.title: Active agent versions in the window (precursor view; canary verdict pending ADR 016 D3)
